days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant city

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Located in the bustling Shinjuku area, this hotel offers modern amenities and is just a 10-minute walk from Shinjuku Station. Perfect for exploring.

timeline.title: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
timeline.detail: Stroll through this expansive garden that blends traditional Japanese, English, and French landscapes. It's a peaceful escape from the city's hustle.

timeline.title: Dinner at Omoide Yokocho
timeline.detail: Experience an array of small eateries in this famous alleyway serving yakitori and local delicacies. A true taste of Tokyo's street food culture.

timeline.title: Golden Gai Bar Hopping
timeline.detail: Explore the eclectic mix of themed bars in Golden Gai, a unique nightlife area, known for its narrow alleys and cozy atmosphere. Great for mingling with locals.

timeline.title: Rest at Hotel
timeline.detail: Return to your comfortable hotel for a restful night’s sleep after an exciting first day in Tokyo.

localTips: Purchase a Suica or Pasmo card for easy travel on trains and buses.
Download Google Maps for navigation — it works well in Tokyo.
Try to visit local izakayas for an authentic dining experience.

days.title: Cultural Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Temples and Markets

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a Japanese-style breakfast at the hotel, featuring rice, miso soup, and grilled fish for a healthy start to your day.

timeline.title: Sensoji Temple Visit
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple, Sensoji, in Asakusa. Don’t miss the historical Nakamise shopping street leading to the temple, filled with local snacks and souvenirs.

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Sushi
timeline.detail: Savor fresh sushi at a local eatery in Asakusa. The variety is vast and the quality is unmatched, showcasing the best of Tokyo's seafood.

timeline.title: Explore Akihabara
timeline.detail: Dive into the electronics and anime mecca of Japan. Visit shops specializing in manga, games, and tech while soaking up the vibrant culture.

timeline.title: Dinner at Izakaya Tsukada Nojo
timeline.detail: Enjoy farm-to-table dining at this izakaya known for its chicken dishes and fresh ingredients. A must-visit for an authentic dining experience.

timeline.title: Evening Walk Around the Imperial Palace
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely stroll around the beautiful gardens of the Imperial Palace. It’s a peaceful way to end your day while viewing historic architecture.

localTips: Plan your visit to popular spots early to avoid crowds.
Use train lines to hop quickly between locations; the JR line is very convenient.
Check the local calendar for any festivals or events during your visit.

days.title: Final Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Shopping and Departure

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy another fulfilling breakfast at the hotel to fuel up for your last day in Tokyo.

timeline.title: Visit Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Experience tranquility at Meiji Shrine, nestled in a forested area. A beautiful place to reflect before heading home. Be sure to walk through the torii gates.

timeline.title: Shopping in Harajuku
timeline.detail: Explore the vibrant fashion district of Harajuku, known for unique stores, trendy shops, and a taste of Tokyo’s youth culture.

timeline.title: Lunch at Gyukatsu Tokyo
timeline.detail: Relish the unique dish of gyukatsu, a breaded and fried beef cutlet served with dipping sauces — a must-try while in Tokyo.

timeline.title: Final Shopping at Shibuya 109
timeline.detail: Wrap up your trip with shopping at the iconic Shibuya 109, a landmark for fashion enthusiasts, filled with the latest in Japanese style.

timeline.title: Head to Narita International Airport
timeline.detail: Make your way to the airport for your flight home. Ensure you have your tickets and passport ready for a smooth departure.

localTips: Shop at convenience stores for snacks and drinks before your flight.
Consider pre-ordering airport transport for peace of mind.
Double-check your flight schedule and terminal information.

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: 10-min walk from Shinjuku Station, famous for the Godzilla head on its terrace.

budget.note: Total estimate for meals over 3 days

urgent.label: Book Sensoji Temple entry
urgent.note: Reserve your spot online prior to visiting.

urgent.label: Reserve train tickets for NRT to Shinjuku
urgent.note: Ensure transport is ready upon arrival.

_warnings.message: Day 3: "Breakfast at Hotel" also appeared on Day 2 — same venue on consecutive days?
